free_text: Study of differential cross-sections of the
Three Si surface barrier detectors with
thickness of 1000-2000 um; set at 10 degree
intervals. The subtended solid angles for all three
SSB detectors varied between 3.5 and 4 msr.
self-supporting target, having a nominal thickness of
75 nm. The determination of the target stoichiometry
and Si:N ratio were accomplished via the analysis of
proton EBS spectra taken at Ep,lab = 980, 1200 and
calculated by standard error propagation based on the
total uncertainty of the Si:Ni ratio
